[**Everything Las Catalinas**](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LzS_4xLQNVi_UfRjRCrU41-9G0xTE-K-gQib2tJ7F3k/edit?usp=sharing) **\- ORIGINAL APRIL 23, 2023**

**Airbnb**

**Description from Casey** 

Casa Seabranch is a luxurious 3-bedroom, 3-bathroom ocean view apartment located in the stunning Las Catalinas, Costa Rica. Designed for the ultimate beach vacation, this fully equipped home features three king suites with ensuite bathrooms, making it an ideal retreat for couples. The third bedroom can also be configured with two twin beds for flexibility. Please note that due to its floor-to-ceiling windows, this home is not recommended for families with young children. Beach Club access can be purchased directly as day passes for $15/day/pp (kids under 8 free).

**Your property- Casey’s edits**

Nestled in the heart of Las Catalinas, Costa Rica, Casa Seabranch is a lavish 3-bedroom, 3-bathroom oceanview apartment, crafted for the ultimate beach escape. With three king suites, each featuring its own ensuite bathroom, it’s an ideal haven for couples seeking relaxation. The third bedroom offers the flexibility to be set up with two twin beds, perfect for accommodating different needs. Please note, due to its floor-to-ceiling windows, this property is not suitable for families with young children.

The home’s expansive covered terrace offers stunning ocean views, making it the perfect place to unwind and take in the sunset. With accordion doors that seamlessly connect the open-concept living and kitchen area to the outdoors, you’ll experience true indoor/outdoor living, fully embracing the beauty of Costa Rica. Beach Club access is available for purchase as day passes at $15 per person (children under 8 are free).

**Guest Access \- Casey**

Guests will be provided the access code to the door and a parking access card for the community parking area to facilitate parking for one vehicle. Beach Club access can be purchased directly as day passes for $15/day/pp (kids under 8 free).

Step into a swanky coastal haven\! Casa Seabranch is a luxurious 3-bed, 3-ensuite bath ocean-view condo in stunning Las Catalinas, Costa Rica. Designed for the ultimate beach escape, this fully equipped home is on the 3rd floor, offering one-level living with breathtaking ocean views, a spacious covered terrace, and steps from Mercado dining and the beach. Beach Club day passes ($15/pp, kids under 8 free). Note: not recommended for families with young children due to floor-to-ceiling windows.

Perched on the 3rd floor, enjoy one-level comfort with a spacious covered terrace perfect for sunset cocktails. **Guest Access- She**

**EASY ACCESS & AMENITIES:**

**🔑 Hassle-free entry:**

* **Door access code provided**  
* **Parking access card for one vehicle in community lot**

**🏖️ Beach Club option:**

* **Day passes available for purchase**

**Everything you need for a smooth, enjoyable stay at your fingertips\!**

**Your property- Owner’s draft**

Welcome to Casa Seabranch, where modern luxury meets Costa Rican charm. Perched on the 3rd floor, this contemporary condo boasts high ceilings, floor-to-ceiling windows, and breathtaking views from every room. Designed for effortless relaxation, the one-level layout ensures no stairs to navigate, while the newly upgraded insulated, double-glazed windows keep the home cool and quiet year-round.

Step onto the expansive covered terrace, a standout feature unique to Casa Seabranch. Unlike narrow balconies, this spacious outdoor haven comfortably accommodates up to 8 guests and seamlessly connects to the open-concept living and dining areas with accordion doors, offering a true indoor/outdoor living experience. It’s the perfect spot for sunset happy hours, dining al fresco, or simply soaking in the sights and sounds of the ocean.

Guests of Casa Seabranch can also enjoy a range of personalized concierge services, from arranging guided tours to booking adventures and wellness experiences. Whether you’re craving a private sunset cruise, ziplining through the jungle, or a rejuvenating massage at the Center of Joy, we’re here to help make your stay unforgettable.

Located in Las Catalinas, a car-free Mediterranean-style beach town, you’ll have access to pristine beaches, 42+ kilometers of hiking and biking trails, and an array of vibrant amenities. Swim, snorkel, or paddleboard just steps away, and explore the town’s cafes, restaurants, and boutiques, all within walking distance. For the active-minded, try beachfront yoga, pickleball, or guided fitness classes at CORE.

With its swanky coastal vibe, modern design, and unmatched amenities, Casa Seabranch offers an unparalleled retreat in one of Costa Rica’s most unique destinations.

Owners notes

Casa Seabranch is located in one of the most unique and breathtaking spots in Las Catalinas, Costa Rica with easy access to everything, dining or amenities, everything is just steps away. 

Nestled between the mountains and the ocean, the hiking and biking trails start just steps from your front door, offering stunning routes for beginners and advanced explorers alike. With the ocean on one side and lush mountain landscapes on the other, this exceptional topography creates a serene and unforgettable setting.

We are situated in Beach Town North in the Mercado area which offers cafes, restaurants, Center of Joy (Wellness services), Core (outdoor fitness classes), Pura Vida Ride (Bike/Paddle Board rentals) and the renowned Copper & Stone gourmet grocery store, perfect for sourcing fresh, local ingredients.

While we are in the heart of everything, Casa Seabranch provides an incredibly tranquil retreat designed for effortless relaxation. Newly upgraded insulated, double-glazed windows ensure the space stays cool, quiet, and comfortable year-round.

Perched on the 3rd floor, it boasts breathtaking views from every room and features a one-level layout with no stairs to navigate. 

A standout feature of Casa Seabranch is the expansive covered terrace. Unlike narrow balconies, this spacious outdoor haven comfortably accommodates up to 8 guests . Experience seamless indoor-outdoor living with our stunning floor-to-ceiling accordion-style glass wall. This expansive architectural element transforms your living space, blurring the lines between interior comfort and outdoor beauty. When closed, it acts as a transparent barrier, flooding the room with natural light and offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. When fully opened, the accordion-style panels fold neatly to the side, creating a wide, unobstructed opening that seamlessly connects your indoor entertainment area with the outdoor patio or garden

It’s the perfect space to enjoy sunset happy hours, dine al fresco, or simply relax to the soothing sounds of the ocean.

Only 45 minutes from Liberia International Airport (LIR), Casa Seabranch is easy to reach yet feels like a world away. With concierge services available to arrange personalized tours, adventures, or wellness experiences, you’ll have everything you need for a memorable and relaxing stay. Whether it’s exploring pristine beaches, paddleboarding on turquoise waters, or hiking scenic mountain trails, Casa Seabranch offers an unparalleled retreat in one of Costa Rica’s most special destinations

AI

## Welcome to Casa Seabranch: Your Slice of Paradise in Las Catalinas

## Breathtaking Location | Unparalleled Comfort | Seamless Indoor-Outdoor Living

Perched between azure waters and lush mountains, Casa Seabranch offers a mesmerizing retreat in the heart of Las Catalinas. This third-floor sanctuary boasts panoramic vistas from every room, inviting you to immerse yourself in Costa Rica's natural splendor.

## Luxurious Comfort

* Newly upgraded, insulated double-glazed windows for a cool, tranquil oasis  
* One-level layout for effortless living  
* Stunning views from every room

## Indoor-Outdoor Living Redefined

Our crown jewel: a floor-to-ceiling accordion-style glass wall that transforms your space. When closed, it floods the room with golden sunlight and sweeping views. Fully opened, it creates a seamless connection to the expansive covered terrace, perfect for:

* Sunset cocktails  
* Al fresco dining  
* Relaxing to ocean symphonies

## Prime Location in Beach Town North

Steps away from:

* Charming cafes and gourmet restaurants  
* Copper & Stone grocery with fresh, local delicacies  
* Wellness services at Center of Joy  
* Outdoor fitness classes at Core  
* Bike and paddleboard rentals at Pura Vida Ride

## Nature at Your Doorstep

* Scenic hiking and biking trails begin steps away  
* Routes for both novice explorers and seasoned adventurers  
* Breathtaking landscapes of forests and dramatic coastlines

Just 45 minutes from Liberia International Airport, Casa Seabranch is your gateway to Costa Rican bliss. Our attentive concierge is ready to curate your perfect stay, from thrilling eco-tours to serene spa days.

Immerse yourself in the magic of Las Catalinas at Casa Seabranch – where every moment celebrates natural beauty, comfort, and unforgettable memories

General overall Notes

Designed with relaxation in mind, Casa Seabranch boasts three king suites, each with a private ensuite bathroom. For added flexibility, one suite can be converted into a twin room, ideal for older children or friends traveling together. Recently upgraded with insulated double-glazed LowE windows, the condo stays cool, quiet, and comfortable year-round.

Step into a swanky coastal haven where style meets comfort\! This modern 3-bedroom, 3-bathroom ocean-view condo is in the breathtaking, car-free town of Las Catalinas. Perched on the 3rd floor, it’s all one level with views from every room. The expansive covered terrace—larger than most—blends seamlessly with the open-concept living and dining areas via accordion doors, perfect for sunset cocktails or ocean-view dinners. Please note, due to floor-to-ceiling windows, this home is not recommended for families with young children.

Enjoy sunset ocean views on the expansive covered terrace and only steps away from the vibrant restaurants of the Mercado area. Beach Club access can be purchased directly as day passes for $15/day/pp (kids under 8 free).

Approved by US airlines and the results are integrated with the NAVICA app on your phone

***All words with a 👉 blue underline  are hyperlinks \- click on them to see more details)***

**Getting There:** Picking up your car rental at Liberia International Airport 👉 [**Video**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=l30fexyzYZ0)

**Closest airport is Liberia (LIR)**  It’s @1 drive to LC. (45 minutes if monkey trail is open/passable considering rain collection). (the other airport San Jose is @4 hour drive to LC)

After you collect your bags, there is a duty free booze store at airport/Liberia 

In Liberia, rental car shuttle services/drivers will pick you up right outside the airport exit door.

If you rent a car, they’ll have a shuttle which takes you from the airport to their office (5 min away).You must have a passport that is valid at least 3 months beyond your stay. 

**Tips (For more Tips click [here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1a-T8r7WJ5ePZysmEohjOjFt8uG5nUeXz92B20UgpqIE/edit?usp=sharing))** 

* **Pack** sunscreen and bug repellant. You’ll need both & they are very expensive in CR. 

* Bring singles and $5 bills for small purchases & tips.

* Download WhatsApp. Commonly used in CR & is helpful in making reservations, etc.

* Take photos of your luggage. Luggage is much easier to find with a photo esp w/the lang barrier

* Pack grocery bags. Stores charge for bags.  Walmart & Pricesmart are right next to airport & easy to pick up stables (meats, cheese, snacks, frozen pizza, beer, wine etc) 

* Bring a folding portable cooler to keep items cool for the 45 min drive to LC (& beach, or boat) 

* **We Strongly Recommend renting an SUV\!** Do not assume your rental will have a USB port to charge phone & Car Play. Check first or bring: A mobile phone mount car holder & USB car adapter that fits into a cigarette outlet

* **Count your bags before you leave the airport**. After you go through immigration, you have to put your bags on an x-ray machine. Then you head outside to wait for your rental van.  When you’re with a group, it’s easy for lots of people to start grabbing bags only to find out a bag was left behind. Before you get into the rental van- double check you have all your bag.

\_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_ \_   
**Transportation:**

**RENTAL** 

It is safe to drive in CR; Waze & Google Maps work well. Main roads to LC and many places you want to go are paved, with occasional potholes.   There are roads to more obscure places that aren’t paved & have many large potholes (they’re fine to travel on, you just need to drive slowly). 

* **Required Liability Insurance fees** \- Regardless of the rental company, **you will need to pay the Mandatory liability insurance which is a CR law**. This will run @ $15-20/day (depending on size of vehicle). \*Note \- *Most advertised prices **do not include** this extra liability cost so make sure you get a quote with all fees added.* 

* **Optional liability fee** \- You can decline CDW (collision damage waiver) as your credit card usually covers this. However, check with your credit card company before you go \- you may need a letter from them to waive this coverage. 

* If you want to be picked up at the airport by a driver & get a rental car later in the week, Adobe will drop you off at Las Catalinas. Concierge can arrange the rental.

[**Adobe Rent a Car**](https://mytanfeet.com/special-deals/get-real-discount-rent-car-costa-rica/) \- Hutchinson’s rent from here. They are the 2nd largest car rental company in CR & in business for 20+ yrs, all cars are 2 years or younger. [MyTanFeet.com](https://mytanfeet.com/special-deals/get-real-discount-rent-car-costa-rica/) has an arrangement with them that provides an automatic 20% off \+ free cooler, basic Sim Card, 2 drivers, road assistance, backseat dog covers, Apple car play. (Here a video of  [the SUV](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XrSmF1nzbxY) we choose)

Adobe Toll Free \- 1 (855) 861-1250  
Adobe Costa Rica \+506-2542-4800  
Adobe Whatsapp M-F only \+506-8494-5956

[**USave**](https://www.usave.com/StarLocationDetail.aspx?fid=250030&loc=Liberia%20\(LIR%20Airport\)-CR). Row’s have used this company. The cars aren’t quite as nice as Dollar but will deliver the car right to the airport and be waiting for you w/the AC on\! Contact [Anibal-amorales@usavecostarica.com](mailto:bal-amorales@usavecostarica.com)

[**Vamos Rental Car**](http://www.vamosrentacar.com) Gary & Amy Dresser rent here. It’s difficult to get the real price – the advertised price doesn’t include a bunch of required charges.

[**Economy Rent a Car**](mailto:mvargas@economyrentacar.com) **\-** [Minor Vargas](http://mvargas@economyrentacar.com%20) Young’s rent here. Call Minor ahead of time & he’ll share quotes w/o all the BS. Tell him CJ sent you

**DRIVERS** \- are also readily available  with advance reservations.

* **Las Catalinas Concierge**  concierge@lascatalinascr.com  

(Luxury vehicle – Approximately $160 one way for up to 4 people)

* **Alex Morena** – Private Driver    	WhatsApp \#   506 8384 8222

* **Euguenia Rodriguez**,  Malekutours    WhatsApp  506 8390 9088

* **Luis** (Young’s usually use Luis) whatsapp \+11-506-6151-7576 

[**\*Javier Vargas**](https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g309244-d3840128-r808923257-Costa_Rica_EZ_Travel_Adventures-Playa_Flamingo_Province_of_Guanacaste.html) ❤🇨🇷🤠-+11-506-8837-1531. A 5 star Rockstar Tour Guide & Driver \- He’s amazing, his excursions are fabulous [LovingCostaRica.com](http://LovingCostaRica.com) (Diane Sagrillo from LC \- Javier is Amazing-he does tours of the whole country) 

Javier Boat Tour \- **Tempisque River** (near Belen)   
2 hrs from LC  *(3 hrs from The Springs Resort & Spa at Arenal,)*   
River is 86 miles long & an important home to diverse wildlife. Picnic and birdwatch along this stretch of paradise. Boat is shaded- plenty of room to walk around.

5am \- Leave from Las Catalina’s.   
5:45 \- stop for light breakfast on the road. (Includes stops along the road since wildlife is active in the morning having breakfast)   
2 hrs \- Motor boat ride along the river scouting for wildlife   
Early Lunch \- outside of the park on a self sustainable ranch run by the local women   
12:30 \- Back at LC at 12:30 PM 

**2 Driving Routes options to Las Catalinas from the Airport**

1. **Monkey Trail Option**:  **This is shorter (approx. 50 minutes**) ***but you have to drive through a river bed**.*  So, it only works in the dry season. We contact LC Concierge and ask if this route is open: [concierge@lascatalinascr.com](mailto:concierge@lascatalinascr.com)  Certain areas of the road can be bumpy & full of potholes.

When you leave the terminal, take Route 21 to the right,  
Continue on 21, cross the bridge over the river, go right onto Route 151 (about 8 km).  
Turn left onto Route 911\.  
You will turn left to stay on 911\.  Look for signs for the Monkey Trail.  
After you make your way into Potrero, just follow the signs to Las Catalinas.  
Once you drive through Potrero, LC is @10 minutes away. After you pass Casa Chameleon (& Sentido Norte) on your left the town of LC starts.  @150 yards down the hill from Casa Chameleon you’ll see a parking lot on your right & an entrance to the reception area on left.  Pull into reception (on left) and a LC hiker/valet will park your car (complimentary service). 

2. **Along the Beach \- This is the longer option** *(from the LC website, approx. **80 minutes**):*

When you leave the terminal, take Route 21 to the right. Drive for approximately 35 minutes, until you reach a streetlight in Belén. Take Route 155 to the right, heading toward Huacas, for approximately 25 minutes. Immediately after Huacas, take Route 180 to the right, looking for Brasilito Beach (15 minutes). Once there, look for the signs for Las Catalinas, passing Potrero village (10 minutes). Continue, going up the hill for 5 minutes, and then down the hill. Arrive at the main entrance to Las Catalinas (GPS coordinates: N10 28.965 W85 47.169).

**Stops on the way to Las Catalinas (or right by the airport)**

**Going East of the Airport**

[Walmart](https://www.weeklycrawler.com/local-news/walmart-liberia-costa-rica-review-prices-and-hours/) \- 10 minutes down the street from the airport \- **opposite direction of LC**. Large, clean with a great selection of items. Usually 10-15% off regular grocery stores. Food, Ice, Beach chairs, electronics, household items.. 

[**Pequeño Mundo Liberia**](https://tienda.pequenomundo.com/)  YouTube link “[What is Pequeño Mundo In Costa Rica 🇨🇷 And What Can You Buy There](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qFigQPpGdsQ)?”

**Sabor Porteno Restaurant** \- If you want to grab a bite before heading to LC, we love this Restaurant (closed on Mondays). It’s 10-15 mins. from the airport.

**Restaurante Tierra Mar** Liberia \- The Dresser’s love . Great ceviche/good wine.  On **Ave 1 between calle 4 and calle 6**.  It’s @10 min. from the airport in Liberia.

**Going toward LC** (West of the airport)

**Discount Italian Store** 160, Guanacaste Province, Nosara.  Name is not inspiring, but they offer great charcuterie (Italian meats & cheeses), cookies, pastas, and wine. They also have an impressive pasta selection including some frozen entrees (Bolognese, frozen meat pockets, and lasagna) that are delicious. **160, Guanacaste Province, Nosara** \- It’s in the shopping center with the Do It Center on Hwy 21 on the right side of the road. Closed on Mondays. Hours 8-6.

Montepulciano D’bruzzo ¢4500/each ($7.14) or box of 6 \= ¢23970 ($38.04...$6.34 each)  
Pinot Grigio Vitis Nostra  ¢4900/each ($7.78) or box of 6 \= ¢23970 ($47.13...$6.74 each)

**Grocery Stores 🛒** \- Onsite grocery @LC is [Copper & Stone](http://copperandstonegourmet.com/) – Amazing- super convenient gourmet store but 2x cost. We recommend you stop at grocery stores in Liberia & stock up on basics   

[**Pricemart**](https://www.pricesmart.com/site/jm/en/club-locations/club/liberia)  \- 3 min away from the airport. Like Costco ($35 annual fee). If you’re staying for a long period, this is worth it. Left out of the airport, toward Liberia; There are markets along the way. 

## [Carnes San Martín](https://carnesanmartincr.com/) \- Located in the Solarium across from Liberia airport, this butcher has a lot of cut options for beef, pork, and lamb. Great way to save money. “This and Pricesmart are the only places I purchase meat from. I live about an hour away but the quality is worth the drive. Place is clean, service is good, and you can find cuts not typical from the regular grocery stores.” 

**Islena \-** [Distribuidora La Isleña](https://di-cr.translate.goog/?_x_tr_sl=es&_x_tr_tl=en&_x_tr_hl=en&_x_tr_pto=nui,sc) \- Great speciality grocery store \- All sorts of delicatessen , wine and fine liquor. European beer, gift baskets, canned food & spices. wholesale prices. Tel: 2293-0609 **(Next to Adobe car rental)** HCC8+PQC, Provincia de Guanacaste, Guardia, Costa Rica

\*[**Sunquest Heli Tours**](https://sunquesthelitours.business.site/) **(Next to Islena)** 

**ACCOMODATIONS:**

**Santarena Hotel** – beautiful rooms (no fridge) with ocean views by beach & great rooftop pool.

**Beach house rental** \- Rent a vacation home (1 \-10 bed). You can rent via the Las Catalinas web site [Beach Town Travel](https://www.beachtowntravel.com/), or directly from homeowners \- typically at a discount.

**Check-in:**

Keys are at the reception desk. They need/will have your info on file if you organize ahead of time.

They will take you/your bags to the home via a golf cart. Maybe request a quick golf cart tour to orient yourself.

LC can arrange daily housekeeping or a “House Mom” who will be there from around 8:00 \-5:00 six days/week to prep breakfast & snacks, do laundry & keep everything clean & tidy – highly recommended\! The wonderful gal we had spoke little English, but was such a treat to have\! You can supply the groceries from your choice store/s to prepare the breakfast & snacks or they can do the shopping at Copper & Stone. Get your hands on a request sheet in which you select ahead of time what you would like served and what time on what day w/head counts. Include any allergies.

**\*Las Catalinas App or WhatsApp \# \+506 8819 9400 to contact LC Concierge**

**App:** To use the Las Catalinas app, you'll need to get a user id and password from the Concierge. You can use it to order food, make maintenance requests, get help with luggage, request housekeeping & many other things.

**WhatsApp**\- \-\* don’t underestimate this service \- You can call directly or WhatsApp to reach the concierge & get quick, personalized answers to whatever you need; dinner reservations, transportation, excursion, luggage pick up, buggy shuttle, umbrella request,...questions, etc. this is a great, easy way to get your questions answered promptly)

**BTW:** Check your bill, occasionally there’s a mixup \- They are service oriented & will make it right.

**Mobile Phone** \- [Mobile Sim Card](https://mytanfeet.com/about-cr/prepaid-sim-cards-in-costa-rica/) \- Kolbi 969G+78W, Provincia de Guanacaste, Huacas,

**Local Restaurants 🥘**

(10% service charge will always be added – additional service tip is not customary.   
AMEX is not always accepted.  A compulsory 13% tax added to everything you buy..

**Newspapers**

[**The Tico Times (English)**](http://www.ticotimes.net/)    
[**AM Costa Rica (English)**](http://www.amcostarica.com/)     
[**The Costa Rica Star (English)**](https://news.co.cr/)  

**On Site Restaurants in Las Catalinas**

* ***Limonada***  

On the beach restaurant/bar in the heart of Las Catalinas.  Open for lunch & dinner with casual, but good sandwiches, salads & entrees.    ***Happy hour daily from 4pm \-6pm*** (buy one, get one on particular drinks).  It’s a wonderful place to gather with friends, meet other town residents & watch the amazing sunsets (bring bug spray). Free wifi.

* [**Pots & Bowls**](http://www.potsandbowlscr.com/menu/english/) *(Link to Eng Menu)*

* ***Beach*** **Club Restaurant ([La Taqueria](https://www.lascatalinascr.com/shopping-and-dining/the-beach-club))** 

Casual restaurant at the Beach Club.  We love the chorizo & grilled fish tacos\! Bring towels. They also offer a happy hour, usually 5-6p buy one get one, margaritas & sangria. Free wifi. 

* ***Pura*** **Vida Ride**

Pura Vida Ride is the source of all rentals of paddle boards, canoes, bikes, etc. It also has amazing coffee (cappuccinos, lattes, etc.) great *empanadas\! &* muffins in the morning and gelato.  

* **Santarena Hotel**   
  *  **Ponciana**  Hotel’s signature restaurant & bar \- Excellent Quality. [**Eng Menu**](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1qDgKvDnRrrl3lbsHO26kIT3XtoUBO_3S/view?usp=sharing) & **[Reviews](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g6437168-d16779914-Reviews-Ponciana-Las_Catalinas_Province_of_Guanacaste.html)**

Indoor & Outdoor seating \- Italian Mediterranean (sourced locally). Social & cultural space where people gather to eat, drink, and socialize. Enjoy breakfast, lunch, and dinner in the restaurant or cocktails at the bar.

* [**Cuatro Calle la Ronda**](https://www.santarenahotel.com/dining/4-calle-la-ronda) is an all-day bakery that offers breakfast (Burrito wrap is amazing), lunch, and snacks on the go. It’s a welcoming and friendly space providing a cozy atmosphere with an outdoor terrace. Coffee time 4-5pm w/BOGO coffees & pastries.

* **Casa Chameleon Hotel** \- [Sentido Norte](https://www.sentidonorterestaurant.com/menu/) – Upscale restaurant/bar with incredible sunset views. Make a reservation well in advance and get there at least 30 minutes before sunset (make separate sunset res & dinner if you wish to stay ) to enjoy “the experience” of watching the sunset. If you don’t go to Sentido Norte for dinner, at least go for the sunset in the bar. If it rains, I would reschedule. Resort attire, adults only. Request a complimentary golf cart in advance for a ride there & back, departing LC from reception. Factor in a 10 min delay. The concierge can make res. [www.sentidonorterestaurant.com](http://www.sentidonorterestaurant.com)

* **Onsite** **Grocery** \- [**Copper & Stone**](http://copperandstonegourmet.com/)**.** Amazing Fresh Groceries, Prepared Meals, smoothies & coffees, salads. Pricey, but convenient. Great fresh juices & fresh breads/pastries. Each day of the week, they offer a vegetarian and a non-vegetarian entrée. Pizza special every Friday

* [Fresh Baked Goods](https://freshbakedlc.wixsite.com/sale/about) Fresh Baked is run by two school girls who live full time at Las Catalinas, Adelaide (10) and Clare (12). They bake delicious treats which can be ordered through an order form & delivered to your home or picked up at a determined location. They hold bake sales once every few months. 15% of proceeds will be donated to a cause determined monthly. [Contact us](mailto:freshbakedlc@gmail.com) 

**Grocery, Shops & Restaurants Outside of Las Catalinas**

**In Potrero** (about 8-10 minutes):

**Grocery**

**MerKdo, Merkado, Mercado Grocery Store, Supermarket, Guanacaste Province, Costa Rica**

* **Super Ceymi** \- small local grocery store F65H+H8H, Provincia de Guanacaste, Potrero, Costa Rica  
* **SuperMercados** \- These are larger groceries & many on the way to Tamarindo   
* **Auto Mercado**; “most American” store we found \- located to the north in El Coco & another in Tamarindo. (LIR to Playas Del, Provincia de Guanacaste, Coco, Costa Rica)  
* **Super Massai Grocery, Brasilito to Potrero, Guanacaste Province, Playa Flamingo, Costa Rica**

* [**La Forketta**](https://qrco.de/bbsW03) – Cozy Italian eatery w/ incredible pastas & brick-oven pizzas, super friendly staff. The seating is mostly outdoors & the restaurant is quite popular so make sure make reservations on WhatsApp  506 8437-5974. Gabby is our fave waitress; she may even bring limoncello shots at end of meal ([Trip Advisor](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g1380092-d14935942-Reviews-La_Forketta-Playa_Potrero_Province_of_Guanacaste.html))

* **Las** **Brisas** – great dive bar atmosphere overlooking the bay.  Relatively cheap beer & wine. Probably better for drinking than eating, but a pretty good bar food type menu. Tuna tower rocks.

* **Carpe** **Diem** in Playa Potrero. Great lunch spot, great vibe, healthy food, outdoor seating,  
* Soda Maria Theresa, and Sol y Mar. \- Pizza places. They’re “fine” but nothing special in the “Italian Village”

* [**The Beach House**](https://www.beachhousecr.com/) – ([Trip advisor](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g1380092-d4747976-Reviews-The_Beach_House_Restaurant_and_Bar-Playa_Potrero_Province_of_Guanacaste.html)) restaurant/bar located on Playa Potrero overlooking the “marina”. Good seafood & American fare. Amazing Tuna Poke Bowl. See full [menu](http://www.beachhousecr.com). Limited parking in the front of the restaurant. Ok to park along the side of the road opposite the restaurant. (Will cook fish you catch)

* **Estero Azul** – a little hard to find, but very local feel. Good food but limited bar, is on the water and we truly enjoyed chilling there. Covered/all outdoors ([TripAdvisor](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g309244-d2217801-Reviews-Soda_Y_Restaurante_El_Estero_Azul-Playa_Flamingo_Province_of_Guanacaste.html)) 

* **Heladeria** (ice cream shop) The name is Heladeria 0039\. We take away extra\!

* [**NASU RESTAURANT**](https://www.bahiadelsolhotel.com/restaurant/) \- Next to the [**Sailing Center**](https://ticotimes.net/travel/costa-rica-sailing-center-potrero-may-worlds-chillest-yacht-club) \- Inside the Hotel Bahia del Sol. Outdoor Restaurant (Covered & uncovered ) loverlooks Ocea. Specializes in seafood & internatl cuisine.

* [**Hemingway's**](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g1380092-d19611372-Reviews-Hemingway_s-Playa_Potrero_Province_of_Guanacaste.html) **Next door to Hotel Bahia del Sol**.Beachside, direct sunset view, sand, Bar, pool, good casual vibe. Sailing club attached. .Gerald's drinks won overall. The signature cocktails eclipsed the food, and not just because there was a good amount of booze in em, lol. Tuna poke was awesome, as were chips and guacamole. Beautiful view and good friendly service

**Flamingo/Brasilito:**

* [**Surf** **Box**](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g309244-d8869786-Reviews-Surf_Box-Playa_Flamingo_Province_of_Guanacaste.html) – Great spot for breakfast & lunch. Food is delicious w/some healthy options. Our favorites include Avocado toast & Medianoche (their version of a Cuban Sandwich.)  See FaceBook page for complete menu. Limited covered seating/all outdoors  
    
* **a'lo Nico \-** a great place to eat local…this is your place. The Casado is out of this world. Also, the lemonade is delish\!\!\! . The chicken shawarma was so flavorful, the Casados were amazing, and the Caribbean and blackberry smoothies were so refreshing\! We couldn’t leave without trying the crepes, and neither should you ☺️ **C6G8+WF5, Provincia de Guanacaste, Playa Flamingo, Costa Rica**

* **Coco** **Loco** –Casual restaurant on beach in the heart of Flamingo bay.  It feels a bit touristy, but worth the trip. Good food & drinks. Park your car on the side of the road near the restaurant and pay “the man” a few dollars to watch over your car.  See menu on their Facebook page. The Coco Loco drink is a treat sipped from a coconut. 

* **Patagonia** **Del Mar** \- Nice restaurant with excellent menú of seafood, steaks, appetizers & nice selection of wines and cocktails.  *Lisa’s fave Tuna Poke* anywhere as of yet. Their sushi is excellent. They also have a happy hour and a pool. Covered/all outdoor eating.  Good idea to make reservations  \- WhatsApp 506-8800-0005. 

* [**Gracia at Mar Vista**](https://graciacostarica.com/mar-vista/) – Amy Dresser’s favorite place. It’s in the Mar Vista “subdivision” (where the La Paz school is located). A gated community, just tell the guard “Vamos a Gracia”. Part of the Gracia experience is access to an infinity pool with a swim up bar. Get there early, enjoy the sunset (in or out of the pool), bring a towel & change of clothes/rinse off, stay for dinner. Excellent menu of nice entrees and a killer key lime pie for dessert. This would be our first choice if you had a large group of 6 or 8 for dinner and no rain in forecast as sunset is key here.  [www.graciamarvista.com](http://www.graciamarvista.com) .  Reservations recommended via website or WhatsApp 506 6110 1687

* **Amigos Tacos y Beer \-** Best Tacos in Playa Flamingo [Next to Super Massai Flamingo, Playa Flamingo 50308 Costa Rica](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g309244-d17416431-Reviews-Amigos_Tacos_y_Beer-Playa_Flamingo_Province_of_Guanacaste.html#MAPVIEW)\+506 8869 3999 [Website](http://www.amigostacosybeer.com/) [Menu](https://www.amigostacosybeer.com/menu)

**Flamingo \- Grocery**  

[**Super Massai**](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/Guanacaste+Province,+Las+Catalinas,+Costa+Rica/Super+Massai+Grocery,+Brasilito+to+Potrero,+Guanacaste+Province,+Playa+Flamingo,+Costa+Rica/@10.4574731,-85.7950277,14z/am=t/data=!3m1!4b1!4m14!4m13!1m5!1m1!1s0x8f9e25ee7d7275cb:0x66fef976ab80fbe9!2m2!1d-85.786591!2d10.4814356!1m5!1m1!1s0x8f9e25421a2ba8fd:0xc0917d9148054f72!2m2!1d-85.782068!2d10.4331484!3e0) \- Best local option (most things on this list are on the main road Ruta 180\)

Fresh fish to bring home in little blue shack on left on beach road into potrero beach

**Brasilito**

* **Masala** **Indian restaurant**  – excellent Indian restaurant

* [**Doki Sushi**](https://drive.google.com/file/d/16e7VNB2OrzRLDLy9i4_lUk_9bouKxgVM/view?fbclid=IwAR05zc8s8Fjil90ckhlymp2BHQkQAKUulmDR7UriTNRHke_nY3SyVgr8obM) \- Excellent take out sushi (they deliver) \+506 6205-3348

**Tamarindo**

* [The Langosta Beach Club](https://www.langostabeachclub.com/)\- If you yearn to escape the tackiness of Tamarindo, drive about 5 min to Langosta. The Langosta Beach Club is a chill place to have a nice glass of wine by the pool or on the beach.  During high season you need to buy a day pass as this is a private club (if you intend to use the pool). We rinse off & change for the evening here. Dina is an awesome waitress.

* [**Villa Deevena**](http://www.villadeevena.com/restaurant.asp) – [MAP](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/Guanacaste+Province,+Tamarindo,+Costa+Rica/652H%2B6PJ+Villa+Deevena,+Guanacaste+Province,+Los+Pargos,+Costa+Rica/@10.2474786,-85.8485709,12z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m14!4m13!1m5!1m1!1s0x8f9e39409203c30f:0xbb189f5e2cc1f893!2m2!1d-85.8371058!2d10.2992746!1m5!1m1!1s0x8f9e471088d76945:0xdc4bb3f502eb5ab9!2m2!1d-85.8207446!2d10.2005784!3e0) *40 min South of Tamarindo* 👀 \- *one of the best restaurants in CR*. Fine dining in casual beach attire. Excellent menu with lots of fresh seafood and an extensive wine list. It’s about 30 minutes past Tamarino/most of the road is unpaved, but the food is worth it\!  There are 8-10 guest rooms at the villa; we suggest you spend the night if you don’t have a driver. The accommodations are basic, but they have a lovely pool, and it's a much better option than driving on the paved road at night.

* [Lola’s](https://www.lolascostarica.info/menu) (famous beach bar) [Menu](https://www.lolascostarica.info/menu) at Playa Negra while you are down that way for a cocktail. Good food & great service. It is a beachfront treat w/a great vibe. **Closed Mondays**

**TENNIS/ PICKLEBALL**

* **Mar Vista** \- clay courts 

* **Tamarindo Tennis club** WhatsApp 8710-0508 [\+506 7075 9002](https://api.whatsapp.com/send?phone=50670759002&fbclid=IwAR09Fptq1ncB0ac1UB15MupfvBkhmgzOfOv76T9xrAgFQd8hD-7BIxx4HBc) 

[200 meters before "Garden of Eden" 50309 Tamarindo, Guanacaste Province, Costa Rica](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?api=1&destination=10.30214473347%2C-85.838472140126&fbclid=IwAR1x0fvbQ76n-xWhMxV6sCH21fAXAfmsijjA2IhFj7muByRFQc8BWJFJ-BY)

*2 great hard courts in the shade most of the day. Open to the public by appt. Cardio tennis and social double's once a week*

*Oliver, the owner & head coach speaks several languages & coaches all levels with equal enthusiasm. You can borrow a racquet, but bring sneakers.*

*Olly is just a world class guy and a world class player \- if you like tennis go and have a lesson you won't regret it as he will improve your game and you will enjoy it for sure*

* **Playa Coco** . Tuesdays and Thursdays 7-9 am $10  
* **Flamingo** \- Margaritavilla tennis courts   
* **Hacienda Pinilla**\- Hard courts (Pickleball Clinic  8-10 am Saturdays   $10)

**Restaurants by the Liberia airport**

* ​​Cuatro Mares \- great chinese restaurant \- [Avenida Central, Liberia 50101 Costa Rica](https://maps.google.com/maps?saddr=&daddr=Avenida+Central%2C+Liberia+50101+Costa+Rica@10.63049,-85.43583) 506 2666 0529 \*Only available for takeout or delivery at this time

**Flamingo** 

[**Super Massai**](https://www.google.com/maps/dir/Guanacaste+Province,+Las+Catalinas,+Costa+Rica/Super+Massai+Grocery,+Brasilito+to+Potrero,+Guanacaste+Province,+Playa+Flamingo,+Costa+Rica/@10.4574731,-85.7950277,14z/am=t/data=!3m1!4b1!4m14!4m13!1m5!1m1!1s0x8f9e25ee7d7275cb:0x66fef976ab80fbe9!2m2!1d-85.786591!2d10.4814356!1m5!1m1!1s0x8f9e25421a2ba8fd:0xc0917d9148054f72!2m2!1d-85.782068!2d10.4331484!3e0) \- Best local option (most things on this list are on the main road Ruta 180\)

Fresh fish to bring home in little blue shack on left on beach road into potrero beach

**Excursions: LOTS** of excursions detailed on the Las Catalinas [website](https://www.lascatalinascr.com/things-to-do%20).  

**Horseback riding**: 

**Pacific Horses** Private tours pacifichorses.as.me We enjoyed an hour ride on the beach in Brasilito and had a guide named Nicole. Wear pants & bug spray. @50$/hr. per person.

**Boating**:  

There are numerous options to spend time on the waters around Las Catalinas. All the options are fun; prices vary based on size of the boats, \# of people on the boat, time of day and the duration of the trip. Definitely try to book a sunset cruise some time during your trip. It’s a special experience to watch the sun sink below the horizon on the water.

* [**Costa Rica Sailing Center**](https://ticotimes.net/travel/costa-rica-sailing-center-potrero-may-worlds-chillest-yacht-club) \- Potrero- boat rentals, boogie boards, paddle boards, snorkel gear & kayaks or hire guide. [\+506 8473 7761](https://www.google.com/search?q=15%20M%20Sur%20de%20Hotel%20Bahia%20del%20Sol%2C%20Provincia%20de%20Guanacaste%2C%20Surfside%2C%2050309&oq=15+M+Sur+de+Hotel+Bahia+del+Sol%2C+Provincia+de+Guanacaste%2C+Surfside%2C+50309&aqs=chrome..69i57.982j0j7&sourceid=chrome&ie=UTF-8&tbs=lf:1,lf_ui:9&tbm=lcl&rflfq=1&num=10&rldimm=16007356624034644877&&phdesc=FRLKr2yrpRg&ved=2ahUKEwidvr6i_obzAhU6TDABHRQSBc4QvS56BAgJEDk&rlst=f#) Amazing views. (15 M Sur de Hotel Bahia del Sol, Provincia de Guanacaste, Surfside, 50309\. Great restaurants next door 

* **Lazy** **Lizard**.  Big catamaran.  Fun, but can be crowded…something like $85 per person for afternoon sunset cruise.

* [**SereFndipity**](http://www.sailingcostarica.com%20) seems like the best “classy” option. Perfect for 6 people. It’s a 50” Yacht, not a catamaran. $990 for ½ day sunset cruise. Pick up Flamingo marina. [Geneva](mailto:Serendipitycharters@gmail.com) handles bookings or Brad 506 8699 5260 

* **Panache Sailing** 506 8495-0039. Sunset catamaran cruise $85 per person or rent the whole boat for $1700. Drinks, snacks, snorkeling equipment included (Panache a bit more comfortable & little more roomy)

* **Pacific** Soul \- sunset trips (Mark \+506 8861 5676\)

* [Mantaray Sailing](https://www.mantaraysailing.com/) is the smaller catamaran option. Young’s enjoyed this for sunset & the morning option is quite popular. They had endless drinks/a delicious lunch consisting of fish and vegetable wraps and a gorgeous fresh fruit platter. Let them know of any food allergies ahead of time. You can drive to the marina to catch or pay $10 total cash per person to and from LC in a van. **The Bali is the more upscale option**. Matteo (+506-8320-5317 [matteo@mantaraysailing.com](mailto:matteo@mantaraysailing.com) ) or Luca usually captains. Private Charters 

* [Sea Star](https://www.seastarcr.com/%20) \- This is Sara Bega’s (Las Catalinas Architect) husband’s business: Sport Fishing, Beach Hopping, Sunset Cruise, Surf Trip (Ask for Michael)

* [Pacific Soul](http://pacificsoulsailing.com%20) Set sail on your Luxury Adventure aboard our Private Catamaran. Each of our tours is fully customized to provide you & your group with an amazing trip touring our magnificent coastline. We have Daily charters with 4, 6, or 8-hour options. **We also have the most amazing overnight adventures that you'll ever find\!** 

* [**Almaco Tours**](https://www.dropbox.com/s/v3vm8appm72yafv/2021%20Almaco%20Brochure%20.pdf?dl=0) (Per Rich Roe \- “*We just had a wonderful day on a private boat out of Coco with Almaco Tours. If you are interested, we highly recommend these guys, I’ve used them 4 times” & Brian “they are great”*.

* **Rolbin** **of Marea Alta Adventures** whatsapp \+11-506-8966-6640. Best out of Coco Beach.

* [**Acqualord**](mailto:acqualordcr@gmail.com) – awesome banana boat & catamaran-well priced \- out of Flamingo beach

**Fishing**:

Fly Fishing tours \- Central Valley (Neil met the owner of this shop) [https://stonemountainoutdoors.com/pesca/](https://stonemountainoutdoors.com/pesca/?fbclid=IwAR0VA4DNubxVM_AjK4erz9jSc_xnwMINl-cJH9ma4z_qSbK3vyvutoCr1zc)

For fishing gear, the closest shop would be located in Brasilito, about 11kms away. The name is La Bahia. There are bigger stores in Coco Beach, about 28kms to the Blue Wave Fishing Store.   
Walmart has some items too, but wouldn't rely on them (in comparison to a US Walmart). 

No license needed.

Cast and retrieve at Playa Danta with a spoon or floating lure. Not much there though. Surf fishing from Potrero Bay is better. Book a deep sea trip through the LC concierge.

Beth Murphy: We always use Sea Star out of Coco but got on a boat at Flamingo.  Owner is Michael Picado.  We have used them 3 times and have no complaints.  They will do pleasure rides with no fishing too\!\!\!  Michael is Sara Bega’s hubby.  
[https://www.wetassfishing.com/](https://www.wetassfishing.com/)  
Skeet Warren on the Bushwacker is also great.  His boat is bigger and very comfortable. It’s a 45 foot betram.  I’ve used him since 2007  
Sarah And Ken Stafford \+1 (207) 400-0251

**Kayak fishing** with [Walter Moreno](mailto:wmorenofishing@gmail.com).  506-6253-9846. Out of Las Brisas at Potrero (5 min from LC).

**Turtles \-** See thousands of Olive Ridley Turtles lay their eggs & watch baby turtles hatch.

**When** \- During the rainy season, Aug \- Dec but specifically October Arribada “arrival by sea”.

Sunset, Early evening & dawn are Ideal. Rough road to get there but an incredible experience\!

**Where** \- [Ostional Wildlife Refuge](https://costarica.org/beaches/guanacaste/ostional/) (located 2 hrs south of LC on the [Nicoya Peninsula](https://costarica.org/destinations/nicoya/))

**Guide Suggestion** \- Jaimy, \+506 8958 8508 “The magic of Ostional” (per Rich Row) 

**Fitness-**

[**Chak Fitness/Core Gym**](https://chak.fitness/%20)  **\-** All Outdoor workouts \- Amazing, onsite, overlooking the beach. Weights made of stones and wood. Chak is owner/trainer. Classes/workout daily for a fee.

[**Yoga at Casa Chameleon**](https://www.casachameleonhotels.com/mal-pais/wellness/) (M-Thurs @9am). $14-best view anywhere.

Beach club gym- indoor gym near pool. make reservations with the concierge.

**Surfing lessons \-** most surfers head to Playa Grande-around 30 minutes away. Lessons available from Jules \+506 8354-2044. Very highly recommended. They also pick up @catalinas.

**Massage \- Las Catalina’s \-** Wake spa (expensive) 

**Massage** with Veronica\!\!  in Playa Potrero. She can come to LC or you can go to her place which has 2 side by side tables, all covered, with privacy,  overlooking the ocean. Many folks at Catalinas love her.  $40. \+506 6108-3491

**Hiking:**

Las Catalinas has wonderful hiking trails right outside your door. The trails are well maintained offering incredible views. 

The concierge can provide a general map, but the notations on the map are limited & the signage on the trail can be confusing.They are working on updating trail signage as I type this. 

Grab a trail map AND download the AllTrails App. The AllTrails app iworks very well in LC. It’ll give you a good idea of the intensity/distance of the hike. Use throughout the hike to ensure you’re staying on the intended trail.

Before heading out, make sure you bring a LOT of water, especially in the dry season. Spray insect repellent on your legs.  Hiking shoes or trail running shoes are a good idea.

To the right of the rocking chairs at Pura Vida Ride, is a hose where you can rinse off your muddy shoes at the end of the hike.

* **Punta Guachipelines-**  is our favorite so far. AllTrails says 4 miles but I got 3.4 on my watch. Easy to moderate. Great vistas.

* **Playa** **Danta** is a short 1 miler. There are a few steep areas, but not too bad. U can hike over and if low tide walk back on the rocks.

* **Las** **Catalinas McHenry**. Easy to moderate 2 miles loop. Trailhead is by the big power poll near Casa Chameleon.  

* **More** of a stroll than a hike, head to the south end of town and go out to Punta Puenca. Good place to watch the sunset. There’s a picnic table & benches at the point**.**

**Medical Emergencies 🏥**

Ask the concierge for help. Closest clinic is in **Huacas-Beachside Clinic**

\+506 2653-9911. Very good care. There is also a hospital in Flamingo

 

**Food Tour Adventures** 😋

[Award Winning Chocolates](https://www.catachocolate.com/) \- Jim Kanter “My friend has a company in Playas del Coco, Cata Chocolate, where they make award-winning chocolates”

[Rancho Avellanas](http://www.ranchoavellanas.farm/#aboutus-section) **Goat Farm** *(22 min SE of Tamarindo)*  
([Tico Times Review](https://ticotimes.net/2013/08/15/the-happy-goats-of-rancho-avellanas-dairy-farm)) ([Insta](https://www.instagram.com/ranchoavellanas/?hl=en))

**Auto [Cleaning](https://www.google.com/maps/place/Stop+Cafe/@10.3250942,-85.8021612,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m5!3m4!1s0x8f9e3a29b178ed77:0x5a8393e09ef7a601!8m2!3d10.3250652!4d-85.7998975?hl=en-US)** Shows up as Stat Cafe but it’s a car wash Stop Cafe Carwash near Villa real 155, Guanacaste Province, Villareal, Costa Rica \+506 2652 9018, [Map](https://goo.gl/maps/y9AhBo88RQ2ggoUz8)  He has an auto brush car wash. He does a really good job.

$5,000 colones for inside and outside clean or 4,000 colones for just an exterior wash. 

**Day Trips**: 

Blogs \- [**Things to do in Costa Rica**](https://mytanfeet.com/activities/50-activities-things-to-do-in-costa-rica/) & [Epic 1 Month Costa Rica Road Trip Itinerary](https://mytanfeet.com/costa-rica-travel-tips/costa-rica-road-trip-itinerary/) (My Tan Feet)

**Diamante Eco Adventure Park, (**30 min from LC) **\-** 350 east RIU Hotel, Playa Matapalo, Provincia de Guanacaste, Playa Matapalo, 50503, Costa Rica (Zipline rides over a forest canopy, plus tours of a butterfly house & wildlife center with jaguars)

**Tamarindo** is a “large” beach town about 45 minutes from LC. It has a large hotel (Hotel Dira), hostels, several small restaurants, art galleries & a lot of little shops. It’s probably the most touristy town in the region & a bit seedy.

It is known for surfing & is a great place to rent a board if you want to try surfing. (Rent boards at Witches Rock). It’s easy to hire an instructor for a quick lesson. Then, grab lunch at Witches Rock restaurant. (Try the BigAss Nachos, Falafel Bar, Green Papaya or Bodega).

* [Tenoria Volcano National Park](https://costa-rica-guide.com/nature/national-parks/tenorio-volcano/) About 2.5 hours north of Las Cat. Contains the stunningly beautiful Rio Celeste. Easy drive. LC offers a one day excursion, but you can book it yourself & stay overnight at the [https://www.riocelestehideaway.com/](https://www.riocelestehideaway.com/). It can be 20 degrees cooler than LC & can rain up there even in dry season. Wonderful hotels nearby, more like ecolodges \-Hideaway and Carolina Lodge

* [Cala Luna Resort](https://calaluna.com/) \- 1 hr \- [World’s Largest Labyrinth In Costa Rica](https://calaluna.com/costa-rican-adventure/worlds-largest-labyrinth-costa-rica/) 

* [Sibou Costa Rica](https://www.tripadvisor.co.nz/ShowUserReviews-g309253-d23291062-r786665387-Sibou_Restaurante-Tamarindo_Province_of_Guanacaste.html) (42 min S)- Stunning Spa & Restaurant with a beautiful ambience off the beaten path gem in the forest outside Tamarindo. Sexy and Sophisticated, feels like the South of France. 

* [Hacienda Guachipelin](https://www.guachipelin.com) 1.5 hrs (close to Rincon de la Vieja) Fun day trip \- Zip Line, tubing, hiking, horses, waterfalls, mud baths, hot springs, wellness etc  Ask to include the oropéndula (hanging bridge to waterfall).

* **LA LEONA waterfal**l (1.5 hr NE)  ([review](https://mytanfeet.com/activities/catarata-la-leona-curubande/))  Rich Diana used [Colorado Adventure tours](https://coloradoadventurecr.com/tours.html#reservation) The tour lasts 2.5 hrs & includes Private entrance, Hike guide, Bottled water, Safety equip lunch, liability policy, $ 25 p/ p (Check prices \- [this site](https://www.nativeswaycostarica.com/tour-item/la-leona-waterfall-tour/) provides more info) Rich “my new fav. As always, a full day trip” 

* [Ostional](https://mytanfeet.com/costa-rica-national-park/ostional-wildlife-refuge-protecting-turtles/)  (2 hr S) \- **Turtle nesting on Ostional Beach** occurs year-round but hits a peak in the rainy season (September – December) (Hundreds of thousands of **olive ridley sea turtles** come to shore to lay their eggs). Night tours are available

**Weekend Getaways \- Overnight travels:**

* **Monteverdi** – (3 hr SE from LC) wonderful 10,500 hectares of cloud forest (7K visitors/yr). National Geographic has described it as “***the jewel in the crown of cloud forests*** (different from Rain Forest in terms of weather, plants, insects, & animals). Definitely opt for a guided hike to appreciate the biodiversity & views (hanging bridges) which are breathtaking. More difficult to get to than Arenal but well worth a visit esp if you have a car.

**Activities:** Hiking, Bird watching, night ecosystem tours (pack rain & warmer clothing)

**Monteverdi** **Restaurants:**

- [Tramonti](https://www.tramonticr.com/php_ing/menu.php)  \- Italian [Beautiful view and delicious flavors](https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g951347-d796972-r798386117-Tramonti-Monteverde_Province_of_Puntarenas.html)  
- [Monteverde Brewing company](https://en.monteverdebrewingco.com/)   
- [Orchid Coffee](http://orchidcoffeecr.com/)   
- [Stellas](https://stellasmonteverde.com/menu/) \- Breakfast

**Monteverdi** **Hotels:**  
[**Hotel** Monteverde Garden](https://www.monteverdelodge.com/)   
**Hotel** [El Establo Mountain Hotel](https://elestablo.com/)  
**Hotel** [Belmar](http://www.hotelbelmar.net/) Located in Monteverde’s cloud forest. 5 levels of [sustainability](http://www.hotelbelmar.net/certificate-sustainable-tourism/) award. 

- **Belmar Restaurants:**  
- **Celajes** \- Farm to table dining with balcony overlooking the Pacific Ocean   
- **Celajes Lounge** (opens at 10am) **Tico Time** (happy hr) (Every day 4pm- 5:30) assortment of “bocas” (the Tico word for tapas), complimentary with house cocktails or our craft beer, as well as specials on house beer & wine.  
- **Farm to Table** \- Complimentary daily tours \- The majority of produce from our organic garden & family farm (called Madre Tierra) is used in [our restaurant](http://www.hotelbelmar.net/restaurante-celajes-sep/)   
- **Cervezas Artesanales-** Enjoy delicious craft beers, a selection of wines, natural drinks and artisanal sausages at La Cerveceria. Join us every Friday, Saturday and Sunday from 1PM to 9PM. Our brewery has become an integral part of [our thriving restaurant](http://www.hotelbelmar.net/restaurant-monteverde-celajes/), drawing beer lovers from across the country. 


  


  


  




**Arenal** **Area**\- 3+ hours East – ***Arenal is the adventure capital of Costa Rica***\-  
**Must visit destination**. *Many wonderful hotels & activities.*  
(Arenal Volcano National Park, Talamanca Mountain Range, Forest Canopy by Aerial Tram or Zipline, Venado Caves-underground wonder-world of limestone channels, a day tour to Cano Negro National Wildlife, Natural Hot Springs, Lake Arenal, La Fortuna Waterfall.

**Rio Perdido**   
[Rio Perdido](https://www.rioperdido.com/) 5 stars \- 1,200 reviews (2+ hr East of LC) (60 minutes southeast of LIR airport) RR

🗺️ Base of the Miravalles Volcano \- San Bernardo de Bagaces, 50401  📞 011 506 2673 3600 

**Hotel:** [Rio Celeste Hideaway Hotel](https://www.riocelestehideaway.com/) 5 Star (2.5 hrs East of LC) (This opulent ecolodge, tucked into pristine dry forest at the foot of the Miravalles Volcano, offers soothing dips in natural hot springs, top-notch mountain biking trails and a palatial restaurant) Beautiful Chic, Ecologically-conscious, Small resort with bungalow rooms surrounded by tropical gardens. 

Adjacent to [**Tenorio Volcano National Park**](https://costa-rica-guide.com/nature/national-parks/tenorio-volcano/), where the amazing Rio Celeste gets its sky-blue color.(1 hr hr drive East to Arenal Volcano)  🗺️ Base of the Miravalles Volcano   
San Bernardo de Bagaces, Bagaces 50401   📞 011 506 2673 3600 

**Ex Tour \- Light Blue River Private Tour with [Javier](https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g309244-d3840128-r808923257-Costa_Rica_EZ_Travel_Adventures-Playa_Flamingo_Province_of_Guanacaste.html)** \- *Can arrange a combo trip such as: Butterfly Garden visit, Guided Tour on a Sloth Sanctuary, Nature Hike to Light Blue Waterfall at Tenorio Volcano National Park, Swim ave prices $100/adult $60/Kids Pick up EARLY- 4:30 or 5:30AM\!*

The centerpiece of the 600-acre private reserve is a unique THERMAL RIVER that runs for over a mile along the base of a rocky gorge. Access the hot river by descending stone stairwells & trails. Many untouched river pools to swim in. The ARCHITECTURALLY AWARDED restaurant has sweeping terraces with views of the canyons, volcanoes & Miravalles dwarf forest. There is a full-service spa, a bar/lounge, and 3 hot mineral pools with floating sun decks and wet bar. There are 15 miles of hiking and biking trails. Complimentary breakfast, wifi & parking. 24 hr concierge services, tour/ticket assistance. (Diana “The hotel is very nice, great food, a really cool thermal river, hiking, spa & other activities- zip lining & white water rafting” “We love it Nancy”). 

These 4 hotels are about 1.5 hrs south of Rio Celeste Hideaway

- **Hotel**: [The Springs Resort & Spa](https://www.thespringscostarica.com/) (3.5 hr from LC) (US \# 954-727-3939) [**CNN Travel**](http://edition.cnn.com/2011/TRAVEL/08/04/hotels.spectacular.views/index.html) voted our 5-star property 1 of 7 most spectacular views in the world. Sprawling [hot-springs resort](https://www.fodors.com/world/mexico-and-central-america/costa-rica/arenal-monteverde-and-the-northern-lowlands/hotels/reviews/the-springs-resort-spa-577761) in Arenal, is the [crème de la crème of luxury lodging](https://www.telegraph.co.uk/travel/destinations/central-america/costa-rica/hotels/the-springs-resort-and-spa-at-arenal-hotel/) in CR. 165-acre property, water slide, signature putt-putt, kids’ game room & our on-site [Club Rio Adventure Center](https://www.thespringscostarica.com/club-rio/), offering river tubing, kayaking, horseback riding, rock climbing, hot springs & amazing Animal Sanctuary.

- **Hotel:** [Nayara Gardens](https://nayaragardens.com/) (5 star) Luxury boutique hotel, widely recognized for providing premium luxury amenities, beautiful grounds & ultra-personalized service at surprisingly affordable rates.  🗺️ 702 road 21007, Provincia de Alajuela, La Fortuna, Costa Rica

(The Springs Resort has more to do on site, but both have beautiful grounds).

- **Hotel:** [Tabacon Thermal Resort & Spa](https://www.tabacon.com/?utm_source=TripAdvisor&utm_medium=ad&utm_term=Website&utm_content=Website&utm_campaign=TripAdvisor)  ([FrogTV 📺](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AnfhBPbW5aY)) ([TripAdvisor](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Hotel_Review-g309226-d293017-Reviews-Tabacon_Thermal_Resort_Spa-La_Fortuna_de_San_Carlos_Arenal_Volcano_National_Park_Provin.html)) world- class cuisine inspired by Dinner in the middle of the rainforest. Inside the gardens is The Spa, a beautiful location & open air bungalows all of them with private jacuzzi (*per Brooke Harris “Tabacon was our favourite\!”“We’re currently staying at Tabacon and LOVE it\! Restaurants, hot springs, and shuttles to activities.” & Doug Monett “Tabacon was our favourite\!”)*

- [BALDI Hot Springs Hotel & Spa](https://www.baldihotsprings.cr/) **\-** 25 Thermal Water Pools Amidst the tropical rainforest & Largest in the world


**Weekend Beach Getaways** 

* **Kintiri  \-** 2 hours from LC, near Nosara. It is 800 meters above sea level and is pure magic, relaxation and amazing food\!\! Staff is super humble and amazing\!\! (All locals). The gastronomy of this place fits right in with the region. Each dish is perfection and tells a story. It’s a couple’s retreat (no children) and dogs are welcome. Prepare for pure relaxation. A new gem in the south pacific of Costa rica\!\! **[https://www.kintiriglamping.com](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.kintiriglamping.com%2F%3Ffbclid%3DIwAR2kUp3Ld8PG13q77Yt-ocgcLV1fTuoIJmRDUjNfMRy7D6JvojNqMk0g6mU&h=AT0GlJmEudoWWcCwmHaPxeDoatnX52yfrWuhWvOTGj6SIpYC1l1sfcnuvBsgI3zLlHlLDAdpv-hstq-EmgO8I9WKyX76uZ2yKgaSwWsmhcixw1e6EoI12Jee3NOArIrfG1kGLGFiSw&__tn__=-UK-R&c[0]=AT3JM09u5_PiOpABWG7tPSmsNU68ipgcsvMOloLlPr4qDZvkH51u3y4m2Zr7ejAu2Jw5sTobqBYoya2sHNsOopRwKcrcTYY4bqpd7zCb4Zs5FGxaanHEJJTGjEfajILXw5WtLMkef8f65LHZfkY3Hx6HdOKS13dTV9Tw5gZF17fB0TU_oMurApRoaXCLFWmEVINQl5Cbh2ENiYE3iWs)**

* **Nosara Beach/Playa Guiones \-** 3 hours south \- a great beach town (known as one of the yoga capitals & 5 blue zones) Beaches free of developments, oxygen rich national parks, and the clearest ocean water in all of Costa Rica with great hotels. Small-town, peaceful charm with plenty of activities 

* **Hotels in the area**

Gilded Iguana, [Nomadic](https://nomadicnosara.com/), [Lagarta Lodge](https://www.lagartalodge.com/en/home.html), [Bodhi Tree](https://bodhitreeyogaresort.com/) and [Harmony Hotel](https://harmonynosara.com/) (hotel owners are the [co-founders of BuzzFeed](https://www.cntraveler.com/stories/2016-01-28/how-buzzfeeds-co-founder-is-protecting-a-costa-rican-town), after a 7 day trip to ‘recalibrate their equilibrium’ spent surfing at sunrise & sunset, catching yoga classes before ceviche lunches & having epiphanic conversations about our lives, and return there every 6 months \- That’s what this place does to people” They had zero interest in becoming hoteliers but bought the run down hotel 15 yrs ago (“to make sure the property didn’t become a garish cartoon that ruined the integrity of Nosara.”) 

* **Restaurants** 

[The Guilded Iguana](https://www.thegildediguana.com/) (Hotel & Restaurant)   
Central location \- offering a large variety on their menu — this is a great spot to come after the beach and continue to relax at by their pool with a pina colada.  
*Per Lotus Ellis “Guided Iguana is great-middle of the action & has a surf club down the road that my kids love. Also like the Nosara Beach hotel \-amazing ocean view & owned by the same people as La Luna & Coyol (love both restaurants) . It's quiet & feels like you’re in Greece”.*

[La Luna](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g656474-d1722395-Reviews-La_Luna-Nosara_Province_of_Guanacaste.html) \- Beautiful Ocean side \- watch the sunset. Delicious pizza, seafood & Mediterranean cuisine.

[Coyol](https://coyol-restaurant.business.site/) (Hilltop restaurant with an [amazing view](https://www.instagram.com/coyolnosara/?hl=en)) Breathtaking panoramas in all Costa Rica. Mediterranean & Costa Rica fusion of Mediterranean well-renowned grilled food.

[Cafe de Paris](https://www.cafedeparis.net/)\- Delicious coffee, pastries & smoothies (healthy vegan/veggie options) 

[El Jardin](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g309235-d23209574-Reviews-Jardin_Tamarindo_Food_Truck_Park-Province_of_Guanacaste.html): Large food truck to walk in & sit down inside. Adorable outdoor seating area

[Rosi’s Soda Tico](https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g656474-d2457116-Reviews-Rosi_s_Soda_Tica-Nosara_Province_of_Guanacaste.html)\- The best, home cooked local food (casado, fresh ceviche). Very affordable

[Seekret Spot Gelateria](https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g656474-d3784740-r214535838-Seekretspot-Nosara_Province_of_Guanacaste.html)\- the Best Gelato in Costa Rica\!

**Furniture**   
Scott of Scott Furniture in Huacas. Custom end tables, coffee tables & bar and kitchen stools  
Bruce \- [Brucescott.scott@hotmail.com](mailto:Brucescott.scott@hotmail.com). He is a lovely older gentleman from Boston who has lived in Costa for 30 years.He also delivers.

Warner Diez \- I found Warner through Demian. He does custom work. I am very pleased with the wood pieces he made for me.+506 8316 8083

[Casa Y Mas](https://casaymas.net/) and [Insta](https://www.instagram.com/casaymascostarica/)  
[Mariloushop](https://www.mariloushop.com/collections/furniture)

**Longer trips** 

# [**Santa Teresa**](https://www.twoweeksincostarica.com/santa-teresa/) **[Beach](https://nymag.com/strategist/article/santa-teresa-costa-rica-itinerary-things-to-do.html)** (4 hrs South- on the tip of Costa Rica’s Nicoya Peninsula)

**Puerto Viejo** de Talamanca (8 hr drive \- can stop  in Arenal for a night to break up the drive ) is a town in southeast Costa Rica, on the Caribbean coast.

**Auto [Cleaning](https://www.google.com/maps/place/Stop+Cafe/@10.3250942,-85.8021612,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m5!3m4!1s0x8f9e3a29b178ed77:0x5a8393e09ef7a601!8m2!3d10.3250652!4d-85.7998975?hl=en-US)** Shows up as Stat Cafe but it’s a car wash Stop Cafe Carwash near Villa real 155, Guanacaste Province, Villareal, Costa Rica \+506 2652 9018, [Map](https://goo.gl/maps/y9AhBo88RQ2ggoUz8)  He has an auto brush car wash. He does a really good job.

$5,000 colones for inside and outside clean or 4,000 colones for just an exterior wash. 

**Replace propane** \- Super China ($77 USD for first full tank then $12 to refill)

San martin

## 

## [**Costa Rica**](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-latin-america-19414068) **\- Capital: San Jose**

## **President: Carlos Alvarado**

* **Population 4.8 million**  
* **Area 51,100 sq km (19,730 sq miles)**  
* **Major languages Spanish (official), English**  
* **Major religion Christianity**  
* **Life expectancy 77 years (men), 82 years (women)**  
* **Currency Costa Rican colon**
